issues.detail: knownIssues[pandas-dead-dependency].evidence was 'pyproject.toml:8' but line 8 is 'requests>=2.32.0'; pandas>=2.2.0 is on line 10. Fixed in-place to 'pyproject.toml:10'.

commands[help].invocation 'python main.py --help' fails with the system Python (botocore.context missing in base environment). Succeeds with project venv (.venv/bin/python main.py --help, exit 0). Bare 'python' invocation is not executable without venv activation, so verified downgraded from true to false. The flags and behavior are confirmed correct.

inPlaceFixesApplied.reason: Off-by-one: line 8 is 'requests>=2.32.0'; pandas>=2.2.0 is on line 10. Content clearly matches claim.
Bare 'python main.py --help' fails without venv activation (botocore.context missing in system Python). Command is valid inside .venv but cannot be verified as written.

issuesFlagged.detail: pandas-dead-dependency evidence corrected from pyproject.toml:8 to pyproject.toml:10 (off-by-one). Fixed in-place.

commands[help] verified downgraded true->false. Invocation works inside project venv but fails with system Python. Recommend adding venv activation note or updating invocation to '.venv/bin/python main.py --help'.

recommendations: Update commands[help].invocation to '.venv/bin/python main.py --help' (or prefix with 'source .venv/bin/activate &&') so the invocation is self-contained and can be re-marked verified: true.

Review dependencies[OliveYoung_MetaData].kind: 'postgres-table' is semantically wrong for a DynamoDB table — schema enum lists 'module | postgres-table | service | config'. Operator should decide whether to update the schema enum or change this entry's kind to 'service'.
